description: Canonical routing benchmark for /do router regression testing. Each test case pairs a natural-language request with its expected agent and/or skill. Used to detect silent routing regressions when models or routing tables change. routing_tier classifies how strictly the router is expected to match: force_route (script must fire correct force_route), candidate (correct answer in top-3), llm_only (no false-positive force-route expected). Two pre-route tiers exercise pre-route.py (the /do deterministic safety net): pre_route_only (must match the expected route), pre_route_negative (must not force-route; idiom guards, D7 planning classes, fallthroughs, and sanitized route-events history patterns).

test_cases.request: push my changes to GitHub
test_cases.notes: Git push intent — force-route to pr-workflow (consolidated)

test_cases.request: push back on this approach
test_cases.notes: Disagreement, not git push — must NOT route to pr-sync

test_cases.request: commit these files
test_cases.notes: Git commit intent — force-route to pr-workflow (commit intent)

test_cases.request: commit to this approach and move forward
test_cases.notes: Decision/dedication, not git commit — must NOT route to pr-workflow

test_cases.request: did CI pass on my PR?
test_cases.notes: CI status check — force-route to pr-workflow (ci-check intent)

test_cases.request: check my logic in this function
test_cases.notes: Code review, not CI check — must NOT route to pr-workflow

test_cases.request: submit a PR with full review gates
test_cases.notes: Force-routes to pr-workflow (trigger: 'submit PR'), but intent is complex multi-gate workflow — LLM should decide final skill stack. Script correctly fires pr-workflow but benchmark expected 'workflow' — now tracked as llm_only.

test_cases.request: clean up my merged branches
test_cases.notes: Post-merge branch cleanup — force-route via 'cleanup branches' trigger

test_cases.request: debug the goroutine leak in the worker pool
test_cases.notes: Go domain + goroutine keyword — force-routes to go-patterns. Previously expected 'workflow' but script correctly fires go-patterns via goroutine/worker pool triggers.

test_cases.request: write table-driven tests for the parser
test_cases.notes: Go tests — force-route to go-patterns via 'table-driven' trigger

test_cases.request: add a worker pool with goroutines
test_cases.notes: Go concurrency — force-route to go-patterns via 'goroutine'/'worker pool' triggers

test_cases.request: wrap errors with fmt.Errorf and %w
test_cases.notes: Go error handling — force-route to go-patterns via 'fmt.Errorf'/'%w' triggers

test_cases.request: review this Go PR for quality
test_cases.notes: Go code review — force-route to go-patterns via 'Go PR'/'review Go' triggers

test_cases.request: work on the SAPCC Go repository go-bits
test_cases.notes: SAPCC Go repo — force-route to go-patterns via 'sapcc'/'go-bits' triggers

test_cases.request: add auth middleware to the Python Flask API
test_cases.notes: Python domain task — python-general-engineer should appear in top-3 candidates

test_cases.request: run ruff and mypy on this Python project
test_cases.notes: Python quality checks — python-quality-gate should appear in top-3 candidates (no force-route fires for 'ruff and mypy' phrasing)

test_cases.request: write pytest fixtures for the database layer
test_cases.notes: Python testing — python-general-engineer should appear in top-3 candidates

test_cases.request: fix the React component rendering bug
test_cases.notes: React/frontend domain — typescript-frontend-engineer appears at position 5 in candidates. Trigger word overlap is weak due to 'rendering' and 'bug' not matching TS agent triggers. candidate_depth=5 relaxes assertion to top-5.

test_cases.request: debug the async race condition in TypeScript
test_cases.notes: TypeScript debugging — needs TypeScript agent + workflow stacking, requires LLM intent understanding. Script correctly does NOT force-route.

test_cases.request: fix the typo in main.go
test_cases.notes: Mechanical one-character fix — quick should appear in top-3 candidates (absorbed fast skill per ADR-192). quick is force_route:true but 'fix the typo' does not match any trigger phrase exactly.

test_cases.request: rename this variable across the file
test_cases.notes: Trivial rename — quick should appear in top-3 candidates (absorbed fast skill per ADR-192). 'rename this variable' does not match the multi-word trigger 'rename variable' exactly.

test_cases.request: add a --verbose flag to the CLI
test_cases.notes: Small self-contained change — quick should appear in top-3 candidates. 'add a --verbose flag' does not match 'add a flag' trigger (substring mismatch: hyphens/spaces).

test_cases.request: extract this block into a helper function
test_cases.notes: Contained refactor — systematic-refactoring appears as top candidate. 'quick' is force_route:true in INDEX but 'extract this block' does not match any quick trigger phrase.

test_cases.request: design a rate limiter feature for the API
test_cases.notes: Feature design phrasing — feature-pipeline appears as top candidate. feature-lifecycle requires exact trigger phrases ('design feature', 'feature design') that this natural-language phrasing doesn't hit.

test_cases.request: plan the implementation for this feature
test_cases.notes: Feature plan phase — requires LLM to recognize 'plan the implementation' as feature-lifecycle. Script surfaces workflow-orchestrator, series-planner, planning but not feature-lifecycle in top-5. No false-positive force-route expected.

test_cases.request: build the feature from the plan
test_cases.notes: State-dependent routing — requires LLM to understand execution context. Script correctly does NOT false-positive force-route.

test_cases.request: run quality gates on the implemented feature
test_cases.notes: State-dependent routing — after trigger narrowing to 'run feature quality gates', script correctly returns no force-route. The correct answer is context-dependent. Tracked as llm_only.

test_cases.request: comprehensive code review from multiple perspectives
test_cases.notes: Multi-reviewer review — systematic-code-review or parallel-code-review should appear in top-3 candidates

test_cases.request: roast this design doc
test_cases.notes: Niche skill requiring intent understanding — script does not force-route. LLM must recognize 'roast' as multi-persona critique.

test_cases.request: execute plan for the API redesign
test_cases.notes: Exact 'execute plan' trigger — force-route to feature-lifecycle is correct for unqualified plan execution.

test_cases.request: execute the plan using subagents
test_cases.notes: 'the' breaks 'execute plan' substring match — no false positive. LLM should route to subagent-driven-development.

test_cases.request: research the topic thoroughly then write an article
test_cases.notes: Multi-skill chain — script fires voice-writer pipeline force-route (via 'write article' / 'write about' trigger), but correct answer requires research-to-article chain. LLM resolves the chain.

test_cases.request: remove AI patterns from this blog post
test_cases.notes: Script fires voice-writer pipeline force-route (via 'blog post' trigger), but correct answer is the private de-AI editing skill. Subtle distinction requires LLM intent understanding.

test_cases.request: deploy the app to Kubernetes with Helm
test_cases.notes: K8s domain — kubernetes-helm-engineer should appear in top-3 candidates

test_cases.request: set up Prometheus alerting rules
test_cases.notes: Monitoring domain — prometheus-grafana-engineer should appear in top-3 candidates

test_cases.request: moderate the subreddit modqueue
test_cases.notes: Reddit moderation task — reddit-moderate should appear in top-3 candidates. reddit-moderate is not force-routed.

test_cases.request: create a new Claude Code skill with quality gates
test_cases.notes: Skill creation — skill-creation-pipeline (wraps skill-creator) should appear in top-3 candidates

test_cases.request: create a new hook for PostToolUse events
test_cases.notes: Hook creation — hook-development-pipeline should appear in top-3 candidates

test_cases.request: go ahead and fix the bug
test_cases.notes: 'go' as verb, not Go language — must NOT route to golang-general-engineer

test_cases.request: debug the goroutine leak causing memory issues
test_cases.notes: Go debug + goroutine — force-routes to go-patterns via goroutine trigger. Previously expected 'workflow' but script correctly fires go-patterns.

test_cases.request: wrap the error with %w for stack preservation
test_cases.notes: Isolates %w trigger without fmt.Errorf as backup

test_cases.request: build a phaser 2d game with tilemaps and arcade physics
test_cases.notes: Phaser game dev — agent resolved via pairs_with (no agent field). phaser-gamedev pairs_with=['typescript-frontend-engineer', ...]. Exercises Step 2 of resolve_agent.

test_cases.request: generate sprite sheet for AI character art
test_cases.notes: AI sprite generation — agent resolved via pairs_with. game-sprite-pipeline pairs_with=['phaser-gamedev', 'threejs-builder', 'python-general-engineer']. First AGENT in list is python-general-engineer (skips skill names).

test_cases.request: add holographic foil card effects with WebGL shader
test_cases.notes: WebGL card effects — agent resolved via pairs_with. webgl-card-effects pairs_with=['typescript-frontend-engineer', 'ui-design-engineer']. First agent in list is typescript-frontend-engineer.

test_cases.request: run a security threat model audit on the toolkit
test_cases.notes: Security threat model — agent resolved via pairs_with=['python-general-engineer']. Single agent in pairs_with, no agent field on skill.

test_cases.request: commit to this design and move forward
test_cases.notes: Decision/dedication, not git commit — must NOT route to pr-workflow

test_cases.request: fish out the root cause of this bug
test_cases.notes: Search/investigate, not Fish shell — must NOT route to fish-shell-config

test_cases.request: give me a quick read on the architecture
test_cases.notes: Brief overview, not a quick edit — must NOT route to quick

test_cases.request: go ahead and merge the branches in your head
test_cases.notes: Cognitive merge, not git merge — must NOT route to pr-workflow

test_cases.request: review the planning meeting notes
test_cases.notes: Reading notes, not code review or planning skill — must NOT force-route

test_cases.request: install the new ergonomic keyboard
test_cases.notes: Hardware install, not toolkit install skill — must NOT route to install

test_cases.request: we need to scan and remove voice samples from the corpus
test_cases.notes: Data cleaning, not voice-writer generation — must NOT force-route to voice-writer (semantic guard exists)

test_cases.request: audit our explanation traces for accuracy
test_cases.notes: Audit/review intent, not generating new explanation — must NOT auto-force to explanation-traces

test_cases.request: what's my current branch?
test_cases.notes: Trivial git status check — router handles directly, no force-route

test_cases.request: compare this approach to the alternative
test_cases.notes: Opinion/comparison — NOT trivial, but should not force-route either; LLM judgment needed

test_cases.request: evaluate https://example.com/some-blog-post
test_cases.notes: URL evaluation — NOT trivial despite looking like a single read; needs Simple+ routing

test_cases.request: give me your opinion on this design
test_cases.notes: Opinion/recommendation — NOT trivial, must not force-route

test_cases.request: create a new hook for the SessionStart event
test_cases.notes: Hook creation — specialist agent should appear in top-5 candidates (lesson #7 preferred-routes)

test_cases.request: scaffold a new agent for Rust development
test_cases.notes: Agent creation — force-route to agent-creator skill

test_cases.request: I need a new pipeline for video processing
test_cases.notes: Pipeline creation — pipeline-orchestrator-engineer must be in top-5 (currently top-1, so pass)

test_cases.request: go test ./internal/... to check the changes
test_cases.notes: Multiple go-patterns triggers (go test, ./...) — high confidence force-route, lesson #7 hard-gate target

test_cases.request: run the goroutine leak check
test_cases.notes: 'goroutine' is a go-patterns trigger — force-route fires correctly, this exercises Go domain force-routing

test_cases.request: create a PR
test_cases.notes: PR creation — pre-route.py force-routes via 'create PR' trigger (multi-word). index-router.py candidate scoring normalization makes this hard there.

test_cases.request: push the work to GitHub for review
test_cases.notes: PR push intent — pre-route.py multi-word trigger 'push changes' or 'push to' should fire. index-router.py candidate normalization disadvantages high-trigger skills.

test_cases.request: do a structured code review on the diff
test_cases.notes: Code review intent — systematic-code-review should be in top-5 candidates

test_cases.request: create a skill that does X
test_cases.notes: Skill creation intent — force-route to skill-creator with toolkit-governance-engineer as executor agent (declared via skill-creator's top-level `agent` field)

test_cases.request: scaffold a new skill
test_cases.notes: Scaffold intent — multiple triggers ('new skill', 'scaffold skill', 'scaffold a skill') yield high-confidence force-route

test_cases.request: build a skill for Y
test_cases.notes: Build-a-skill intent — force-route fires on single trigger 'build a skill' (medium confidence)

test_cases.request: fact check this article before we publish
test_cases.notes: Direct trigger 'fact check' — fact-check appears in top-3 candidates (not force-routed)

test_cases.request: verify the claims in this draft against the sources
test_cases.notes: Direct trigger overlap with 'verify claims' — fact-check is top candidate

test_cases.request: make sure nothing in this draft is wrong before I post it
test_cases.notes: Zero trigger keywords — LLM must recognize pre-publish claim verification as fact-check. Script correctly does not force-route.

test_cases.request: check if these unit tests cover the facts table
test_cases.notes: 'facts' is a database table, not claim verification — must NOT route to fact-check

test_cases.request: give me headline options for this post
test_cases.notes: Direct trigger 'headline' — headlines is top candidate (not force-routed)

test_cases.request: draft a subject line for the newsletter
test_cases.notes: Direct trigger 'subject line' — headlines is top candidate

test_cases.request: what should I call this piece so people actually click it
test_cases.notes: Zero trigger keywords — LLM must recognize title-finding intent as headlines. Script correctly does not force-route.

test_cases.request: summarize the headline figures from the quarterly report
test_cases.notes: 'headline figures' means top-line numbers, not titles — must NOT route to headlines

test_cases.request: collect news items for tomorrow's briefing
test_cases.notes: Direct trigger 'collect news' — news-collection is top candidate (not force-routed)

test_cases.request: run news triage on this morning's feed
test_cases.notes: Direct trigger 'news triage' — news-collection is top candidate

test_cases.request: pull together what happened this week in the industry and keep only the recent stuff
test_cases.notes: Zero trigger keywords — LLM must recognize collect-and-freshness-filter intent as news-collection. Script correctly does not force-route.

test_cases.request: collect the news about our test failures from CI logs
test_cases.notes: Gathering CI failure context, not a news pipeline — must NOT route to news-collection ('the' breaks the 'collect news' trigger)

test_cases.request: push back on this design
test_cases.notes: Disagreement idiom, not git push - pre-route must not force-route (guard word 'back')

test_cases.request: fish out the bug
test_cases.notes: Search idiom, not the Fish shell - phrase guard 'fish out' must suppress

test_cases.request: commit to a decision
test_cases.notes: Resolve idiom, not git commit - pre-route must not force-route

test_cases.request: merge ideas in your head
test_cases.notes: Thinking idiom, not git merge - phrase guard 'merge ideas' must suppress

test_cases.request: commit to a decision by friday
test_cases.notes: Deadline idiom, not git commit - pre-route must not force-route

test_cases.request: push back on the proposed architecture
test_cases.notes: Disagreement idiom, not git push - guard word 'back' must suppress

test_cases.request: merge these ideas into one proposal
test_cases.notes: Synthesis idiom, not git merge - phrase guard 'merge ideas' must suppress

test_cases.request: ship of theseus argument about identity
test_cases.notes: Philosophy idiom, not shipping code - phrase guard 'ship of theseus' must suppress

test_cases.request: review my essay draft
test_cases.notes: Prose review, not a code/PR review - guard word 'essay' must suppress

test_cases.request: publish a paper on distributed consensus
test_cases.notes: Academic publishing, not publishing code - phrase guard 'publish a paper' must suppress

test_cases.request: make the parser thread safe
test_cases.notes: Concurrency mechanics, not a security review - 'code safe' guard word 'thread' must suppress

test_cases.request: break this function into smaller pieces
test_cases.notes: Refactor phrasing - 'break into' routes to security-review only with an intrusion companion

test_cases.request: upload my work to google drive
test_cases.notes: File sync, not version control - phrase guard 'google drive' must suppress

test_cases.request: continue debugging the flaky integration test
test_cases.notes: D7 class 'continue': ordinary work continuation, not a plan-lifecycle request

test_cases.request: resume playback where the video stopped
test_cases.notes: D7 class 'resume': media control, not a plan-lifecycle request

test_cases.request: pause the batch job while we investigate
test_cases.notes: D7 class 'pause': process control, not a plan-lifecycle request

test_cases.request: unsure whether this cache is stale
test_cases.notes: D7 class 'unsure': uncertainty about a fact, not a plan-lifecycle request

test_cases.request: hand off the request to the load balancer
test_cases.notes: D7 class 'handoff': traffic routing, not a session handoff

test_cases.request: explain how the scheduler picks the next job
test_cases.notes: Explanation request with no trigger keywords - must fall through to semantic routing

test_cases.request: what does this regex match
test_cases.notes: Quick question with no trigger keywords - must fall through

test_cases.request: rename the helper for clarity
test_cases.notes: Small edit with no trigger keywords - must fall through

test_cases.request: summarize the differences between these two configs
test_cases.notes: Comparison request with no trigger keywords - must fall through

test_cases.request: root-cause the ranking bug in the report
test_cases.notes: History pattern: debugging requests route semantically (systematic-debugging); pre-route must not force-route

test_cases.request: go live with the feature flag rollout
test_cases.notes: Feature launch idiom, not a web deploy - 'go live' needs a deploy companion word

test_cases.request: commit these changes and push to origin
test_cases.notes: Commit+push intent must force-route to pr-workflow so git quality gates always run

test_cases.request: git push my branch and open a pull request
test_cases.notes: Push+PR intent must force-route to pr-workflow

test_cases.request: check whether CI went green on my pull request
test_cases.notes: CI status on a submitted change - supplemental trigger 'went green' must fire

test_cases.request: push my work, open a PR, and watch the checks
test_cases.notes: History pattern (push, PR, CI, merge requests): must force-route to pr-workflow

test_cases.request: stage and commit my changes
test_cases.notes: Stage+commit intent must force-route to pr-workflow

test_cases.request: submit my changes for review
test_cases.notes: Paraphrased git intent - supplemental trigger 'submit changes' must fire

test_cases.request: run a security review on the upload handler
test_cases.notes: Explicit security review must force-route to security-review

test_cases.request: check whether anyone could break into this server
test_cases.notes: Intrusion phrasing - 'break into' with companion 'anyone' must force-route

test_cases.request: did I leave any passwords or keys in the code
test_cases.notes: Secret-leak check - supplemental trigger 'passwords keys' must fire

test_cases.request: scan this diff for security vulnerabilities
test_cases.notes: Vulnerability scan must force-route to security-review

test_cases.request: is this endpoint code safe to expose
test_cases.notes: Safety question about code - 'code safe' trigger with no mechanics guard word must fire

test_cases.request: resume the plan for the schema migration
test_cases.notes: D7 positive class: plan resume must keep force-route (new phrasing; originals in test_pre_route_planning.py)

test_cases.request: pause the plan until the review lands
test_cases.notes: D7 positive class: plan pause must keep force-route

test_cases.request: create a plan for splitting the monolith
test_cases.notes: D7 positive class: plan creation must keep force-route

test_cases.request: run the go tests for the parser package
test_cases.notes: History pattern (Go test/fix traffic): go test intent must force-route to go-patterns
